title: NPC Physical Traits
description: A distinctive physical feature or quality to make an NPC immediately memorable.

entries.result: A long beard with intricate braids, each one representing something the NPC refuses to explain.
A cleft cut into one side of their nose, old and well-healed.
Tiny tattoos crawling from the corner of their mouth to the edge of their eye — dozens of them, each a different symbol.
One arm noticeably shorter than the other, with a different shape of hand at the end.
A missing front tooth they make no effort to hide.
Perpetual bags under their eyes, deep and bruised-looking regardless of how much sleep they claim to have gotten.
Meticulously groomed — every hair in place, beard trimmed to the millimeter, clothes unwrinkled.
An array of mismatched jewelry, clearly collected over years rather than chosen to match.
Scars and thick callouses running up both forearms, the kind built by a lifetime of repetitive hard work.
Long, unusually slender fingers that seem slightly too long for their hands.
Noticeably well-developed leg muscles visible even through their clothing.
A broad, flat nose that has clearly been broken more than once.
Extremely pale skin with a faint blue tint to the veins visible at their temples and wrists.
A jaw so pronounced it seems almost architectural — square and heavy.
One ear slightly higher than the other, a flaw so minor it takes a moment to identify why their face looks slightly off.
Deeply tanned skin on their hands and face but pale everywhere else — the tan of someone who works outdoors.
A scar that bisects one eyebrow cleanly, leaving a thin gap in the hair.
Cheekbones so pronounced they cast small shadows on the face in direct light.
A forehead marked with a faded brand, old enough that the edges have blurred.
Extremely long eyelashes, more notable because nothing else about their face is delicate.
A port-wine birthmark covering the left side of their jaw and neck.
One cheek slightly puffier than the other from an old injury that never healed quite cleanly.
A face that is deeply, dramatically symmetrical in a way that feels slightly uncanny.
Laugh lines so deep they're visible even when the person isn't smiling.
A thin, almost lipless mouth that gives their face a severe, compressed look.
A widow's peak so sharp it looks almost deliberate.
Deep-set eyes that sit in shadow even in good light, giving them a permanent look of quiet calculation.
A round, open face that makes them look younger than they are — something they seem aware of and either play up or resent.
Freckles so dense they nearly form a single patch of color across the nose and cheeks.
An unusually large and mobile mouth, given to very expressive shapes even when they're not speaking.
A long, thin scar running vertically across their lips.
Slightly protruding ears, pinned close to the head by habit and perhaps embarrassment.
Skin that tans in minutes — dark and even, with no signs of burning.
A complexion that flushes intensely with any strong emotion, betraying them constantly.
A nose that dominates their face — long and curved, the first thing you notice.
Three small dots tattooed beneath their left eye in a triangle, faded with age.
A gap between their two front teeth wide enough to be notable.
Heavy, expressive eyebrows that do most of the emotional work in their face.
A receding chin they compensate for with very confident posture.
An unusually wide forehead that somehow suits the rest of their face perfectly.
Faint parallel scars along their right cheek — three lines, old and nearly invisible.
A face that has clearly been reconstructed after some significant injury — the proportions are slightly off.
Very thin, arched eyebrows that look maintained but probably aren't.
A cauliflower ear on the left side, legacy of too many blows in too many fights.
Dark circles under their eyes that look permanent and constitutional rather than from lack of sleep.
A neck tattoo, visible above their collar, the design partially hidden.
A small round scar at the center of their forehead, perfectly circular.
Crow's feet at the corners of their eyes that appear when they squint, which is often.
Thin lips with a very defined cupid's bow — precise and a little imperious looking.
A face that seems younger in repose and older when animated — the expression adds years.
Eyes of two different colors — one warm brown, one pale grey.
Very light blue eyes that look almost colorless in bright light.
Eyes so dark brown they appear black unless examined closely in direct light.
A visible ring of gold around the pupil that gives their eyes an unusual warmth.
One eye that wanders slightly — not a full cast, just enough to make sustained eye contact faintly disorienting.
Pale green eyes with a dark ring around the iris.
Eyes that are larger than expected for their face, giving them a perpetually alert expression.
Deep-set eyes of an amber color uncommon enough to notice.
Very small, sharp eyes that seem to take in more than they let on.
Eyes ringed with dark lashes so thick they look artificially applied.
A pale, milky eye on the left side — blind, and they neither hide nor draw attention to it.
Eyes with unusually large pupils that give the impression of permanent low-light adaptation.
Hooded eyes that make them look perpetually drowsy, which is at odds with their energy.
A prominent sclera — more white visible than usual, giving their gaze an unintentional intensity.
Eyes that change apparent color depending on the light — grey indoors, blue-green in sunlight.
Very red-rimmed eyes, chronic and constitutional rather than from recent emotion.
Eyes crinkled at the corners from years of squinting into wind or sun.
An unusual downward cant to the outer corners of their eyes that reads as either sad or kind depending on their expression.
Startlingly bright eyes in a shade that seems too vivid for their otherwise weathered face.
Eyes that never quite land on you directly — they look slightly past you, consistently.
A mouth that defaults to a slight frown at rest — not unhappy, just architecturally severe.
A nose with a sharp upturn at the tip that gives their profile a distinctive silhouette.
Full lips, dramatically so, that make their face oddly expressive even from across a room.
A mouth that defaults to a faint smile even in neutral expression — unsettling in serious moments.
A nose that was once aquiline and is now flattened across the bridge from an old break.
Very thin lips, barely distinguishable from the surrounding skin in poor light.
A distinctive nasal quality to their voice suggesting a structural issue with their sinuses — present since birth, they say.
A wide, mobile mouth that stretches nearly ear to ear when they really laugh.
A nose with wide nostrils that flare visibly when they're interested or annoyed.
A mouth that always looks slightly dry — they lick their lips when nervous.
A completely shaved head with the ghost of a very dark hairline still visible at the skin.
A single long braid hanging from an otherwise closely-cropped head, reaching the middle of their back.
Hair that is aggressively, completely grey despite a relatively young face.
Extremely coarse, dense hair kept short but resistant to lying flat.
Dramatic dark hair with a sharp widow's peak that makes their forehead look taller than it is.
Hair worn loose and long, streaked with natural pale highlights that look almost deliberate.
A shaved side and long top, heavily oiled and swept back.
Thin, fine hair worn in an elaborate updo that must take considerable time to achieve.
A close-cropped beard and mustache so precisely shaped they must be maintained daily.
Long, thick locs worn pinned up with several hand-carved pins.
Completely white hair, worn naturally and without apparent self-consciousness.
Hair that curls tightly near the roots but loosens dramatically by the ends.
A patchy beard that looks intentional enough to suggest this is simply how it grows.
Hair tucked entirely under a well-worn hat — you suspect you will never see it.
Extremely long hair worn loose, clearly taken care of, clearly a point of some pride.
Hair unevenly grown back from a recent illness or wound — the shape of the original cut is just visible.
A dramatic streak of pure white through otherwise dark hair, old enough to have been there for years.
Hair cropped so close it shows the contours of their skull, including an interesting dent above the left ear.
A very thick, full beard that dominates the lower half of their face entirely.
Hair that falls across one eye that they never seem to move, leaving you constantly uncertain what that eye is doing.
Exceptionally tall — not just tall, but tall enough that they duck through most doorframes out of habit.
Very short but with a physical presence and bearing that makes the height feel irrelevant.
A heavyset build worn with complete ease — they move comfortably and unselfconsciously in their body.
Lean to the point of gauntness, but not sickly — just very little surplus anywhere.
A barrel chest that makes them look wider than they are tall when seated.
Extremely broad shoulders that make their neck look shorter than it probably is.
Long arms relative to their height — their hands hang nearly to their knees when standing.
A noticeable limp they've long since compensated for — it's in the rhythm of how they walk, not a stumble.
The posture of someone who spent years in formal service — upright, controlled, never fully casual even now.
The posture of someone who has spent decades hunched over a worktable — a permanent forward lean.
Hands much larger than expected for their frame — thick-fingered and rough.
Unusually small hands for their body, kept very clean with carefully maintained nails.
A spine that has settled into a mild sideways curve — old, and not painful anymore, they say.
A missing finger on the left hand — the ring finger, gone at the second knuckle.
Extra weight carried low and forward, with the careful deliberate gait of someone who has learned how to move with it.
A build that looks soft until they move — then the efficiency of it becomes apparent.
A significant height difference between one shoulder and the other, noticeable when they're still.
Hands that shake slightly and constantly — not from fear, just always.
A body that has been very strong and is now in late middle age — the shape of old muscle still present.
Extremely precise, economical movements — they never gesture unnecessarily and always land exactly where they intend.
They never sit still — a constant low-level fidget of the fingers or the feet.
They stand with their weight on one hip, always the same hip.
They maintain careful, deliberate eye contact — more than is quite comfortable.
They avoid eye contact almost entirely, addressing a point slightly above or beside your head.
They laugh too loudly for indoor spaces and seem unaware of this.
They speak with their hands — not gesturing, but actually touching things and people when they talk.
They pause for an unusual length of time before responding to any question.
They tilt their head slightly when listening, in a way that makes them look like a curious animal.
They drum their fingers when waiting, in a pattern that suggests they're counting something.
Their voice is significantly quieter than expected for their physicality — you have to lean in.
They smell strongly of a specific thing — woodsmoke, machine oil, salt water, old iron, or something herbal.
A walk that takes up more space than it needs to — broad strides, arms swinging wide.
They touch their face frequently when thinking, specifically the left side of their jaw.
Whenever they stop moving, they immediately cross their arms — clearly a default resting state.
They blink slowly and infrequently in a way that becomes noticeable once you notice it.
A habit of straightening things around them — objects on a table, their own clothes — without seeming to realize they're doing it.
They crack their knuckles at the start of any serious conversation.
They stand too close — not aggressively, just seemingly unaware of conventional spacing.
They breathe audibly through their nose, a faint whistle on the inhale.
They rarely change their facial expression, which makes the moments they do considerably more significant.
A long scar running from their left ear to the point of their chin, old and silver.
A burn covering most of their right forearm and hand — long healed but extensive.
A series of small round scars up their left arm at regular intervals — they don't discuss these.
A professionally done tattoo sleeve on one arm, detailed and clearly costly when it was commissioned.
A poorly done tattoo on their neck, the kind done by a friend rather than a professional.
A scar across both lips — horizontal, thin, and clearly from a blade.
A brand on the inside of their left wrist, faded but still legible as a number or symbol.
Prominent veins on the backs of their hands, vivid and close to the surface.
Nails kept very short and clean — the nails of someone for whom cleanliness has professional consequences.
Nails worn down to nearly nothing from habit — not bitten but pressed and ground away.
A chunk missing from their right ear, taken cleanly, long healed.
Three fingers on the right hand ring-scarred — the marks of someone who has worn heavy rings for decades.
A faded ink stain on the first two fingers of their right hand that will never fully wash out.
Hands calloused in the specific places that come from extended use of a particular tool.
A prosthetic hand on the left side, clearly functional and clearly old — they use it without thinking.
A scar on their throat, thin and horizontal, that gives their voice a slight rasp.
Paint under their nails and in the creases of their knuckles that never fully comes out.
Silvery hypopigmented patches on the forearms and neck — old sun damage or scarring, long integrated.
A finger that sits at a slightly wrong angle, healed from a break that was never properly set.
A surgical scar visible above their collar or cuff edge — larger than a simple procedure would explain.
They wear too many rings — both hands, most fingers — and they all look like they mean something.
A single piece of jewelry worn visibly and clearly never removed, worn smooth by years of contact.
Clothing that is clean and in good repair but of a noticeably older style.
They dress simply and practically but one item is conspicuously expensive.
A hat they wear in all circumstances and resist removing even indoors.
Gloves worn indoors — not cold weather gloves, just always gloved.
Clothing selected for function over form, patched and maintained but not replaced.
A very distinctive belt or baldric — the one item they have clearly spent real money on.
They carry several visible tools clipped or tied to their person — more than the current situation explains.
Clothing bearing the colors or insignia of an organization that no longer exists.
A single earring, heavy and old, that sways when they turn their head.
Boots that cost more than everything else they're wearing combined.
A well-worn traveling cloak worn in every weather, clearly long-favored — the hem replaced but the rest original.
Very fine clothes worn with no apparent concern for the rough environment they're currently in.
Practical, nondescript clothing — the kind worn specifically not to be remembered.
They look ten years younger than they are and clearly know it.
They look older than they are — not poorly, just very lived-in.
A slight tremor in their hands that is recent enough that they're still quietly adjusting to it.
Moving more carefully than someone their age should need to — an old injury that has not aged well.
The easy physicality of someone who has remained active their entire life.
Deeply weathered skin, lined and spotted from decades outdoors.
Still strong in the way of someone who was very strong when young — not what they were, but not gone either.
A persistent cough they minimize — present at the back of everything they say.
They tire mid-sentence occasionally and then pick back up — something systemic, long-present.
A gait that shows the history of an old leg injury more clearly on cold days.
They are quite beautiful in a way that seems to make them genuinely uncomfortable.
A face that is striking without being conventionally attractive — too much of everything to be handsome, too interesting to look away from.
A very particular stillness to their face that reads first as calm and later as watchfulness.
A quality of constant slight amusement that is either genuine or so practiced it amounts to the same thing.
Something in how they move says they've been hit before and know exactly how to fall.
A pleasant face that becomes difficult to describe immediately after looking away from it.
A quality of taking up exactly the right amount of space — not imposing, not self-effacing, just present.
A face that reads as completely open and trustworthy, which is either genuine or extremely concerning.
An air of having recently recovered from something that left its mark — still not quite at full capacity, but almost.
Something in their hands says manual skill of a very specific kind — the way they pick things up, set things down.
A voice that carries precisely as far as they intend it to and no further.
An unusual height proportion — very long torso relative to legs, only apparent when they're seated.
Skin on their forearms that shows the fine pale lines of very old, very numerous small cuts.
They carry themselves with the physical confidence of someone who has rarely needed to fight because they rarely lose.
An old wound that affects their right arm — they compensate so naturally you only notice on very close observation.
Teeth that are very white and very even — not natural, clearly maintained.
Teeth that are natural and imperfect — one slightly overlapping another, one slightly turned.
Hair that is always slightly damp, as if they recently washed it or were recently somewhere humid.
A ring on their right middle finger they rotate when thinking — a constant, unconscious motion.
The callous pattern of someone who has used a bow for many years — three fingertips on the right hand worn smooth.
Knuckles that have been split and re-split so many times the skin there is different in texture.
A resting expression that reads as deeply polite interest and gives nothing else away.
They have clearly shaved recently but not quite recently enough — a fine regrowth visible in certain light.
Clothing that fits poorly in a specific way — too large in the shoulders, or the sleeves slightly too short.
A slight asymmetry to their face that you cannot quite locate the source of.
A very particular way of sitting — always with one foot flat on the floor, one leg crossed.
They tap three times on any surface before writing on it.
Their handshake is very careful — controlled pressure, exactly long enough, then done.
A mole or cluster of moles at the hairline, visible when they pull their hair back.
A thumb that extends backward at an unusual angle — hypermobile, and they use it in conversation to make a point.
They gesture primarily with one hand while the other remains completely still.
A faint rhythmic tapping of their foot, slow enough that you aren't sure if it's intentional.
A chin they jut forward slightly when listening — as if moving toward something.
An unusually mobile forehead — every thought registers there briefly before the rest of their face decides what to do.
The hands of someone who has worked with fiber, thread, or rope for years — fingertips worn smooth in a particular way.
They touch the side of their neck when uncertain — a tell so consistent you can set your watch to it.
Very clean clothing despite circumstances that should make it otherwise.
Very dirty hands despite circumstances that should make it otherwise.
A stitch of grey at both temples and nowhere else.
Laughter that comes in short bursts that stop abruptly rather than trailing off.
They orient their body toward exits without apparently being aware of this.
Very deliberate enunciation — no swallowed syllables, no contractions, as if they're being transcribed.
A walking pace just slightly faster than feels comfortable for most people to match.
Boots with the heels worn uneven — more weight on the outside of one foot, the inside of the other.
They smell faintly of smoke older than today — not current fire, something that has lived in their clothing for years.
A quality of listening that suggests they're hearing everything including what you're not saying.
A slight lateral sway when they're standing still that looks involuntary.
Hands that are always moving when not engaged — folding, touching a thumb to each finger in sequence.
A habit of standing with their back against something when the option is available.
The specific competent calm of someone who has dealt with something worse than whatever this currently is.
A quality of wearing their face carefully in certain situations and relaxing it noticeably when those situations pass.
They seem to be keeping track of everyone in the room without looking like they're doing so.
A faded tattoo on the back of their left hand, the design no longer fully legible.
Hands that are cold — not just cool, actively cold, regardless of ambient temperature.
Very warm hands, notably so when they make contact — constitutionally warm rather than feverish.
A pattern of small raised scars on their scalp, visible when they push their hair back.
They blink deliberately before answering difficult questions, as if resetting.
A habitual slight nod when you speak to them — not agreement, just acknowledgment, but easily mistaken for agreement.
Slightly sunken cheeks that suggest weight lost over time rather than natural proportion.
Forearms wrapped in linen or leather, reason not offered, never commented on.
They chew the inside of their cheek when thinking — the jaw working subtly, visibly.
A pale line at the wrist where something was once clasped for a very long time.
A build that was once significantly larger — the clothes, the movement, the proportion all speak to it.
A very faint accent that surfaces only in certain words or when they're tired.
They crack their back reflexively when they stand from sitting — a full-spine adjustment, without comment.
A small hard lump visible at their collar or cuff edge — something worn against the skin.
Ink stains on their right thumb and the side of their hand at the base of their pinky.
A habit of greeting people without looking at them first — they finish what they're doing, then turn, fully.
Their teeth are very slightly too large for their mouth — not grotesquely, just giving their face a slightly intense quality at certain angles.
They peel at the skin on their thumb when nervous, completely habitually.
They carry something in their right hand when walking — turning it over, pocketing it when they stop.
A habit of pressing their lips together before speaking that reads either as deliberation or suppression.
They look younger when animated and suddenly, remarkably older when they go still.
A nose with a fine network of broken capillaries across the bridge — constitution or habit, unclear.
A quality of giving you their complete attention that is either flattering or overwhelming depending on the moment.
They breathe through their mouth when concentrating — silent but open.
A habit of tracking movement at the edge of their vision even in conversation — eyes flicking to it and back.
Clothing with small careful alterations done by hand — seams let out, a collar replaced, sleeves lengthened.
They touch their collarbone when surprised — a fast, unconscious reach that stops short of whatever it was reaching for.
Very defined, strong forearms that taper to smaller hands than you'd expect from them.
A specific way of looking at things they want — a brief complete inventory before their expression returns to neutral.
Hair that is clearly not quite its original color anymore, the transition handled quietly rather than dramatically.
A permanent squint that is probably sun damage, giving them a look of permanent mild skepticism.
Very reluctant to sit down — given the option they stand, and given no option they sit forward.
A ring of callous around each wrist, old and well-integrated — the mark of something worn for a very long time.
A quality of physical comfort with themselves that is neither vanity nor indifference — they exist in their body without argument.
A very faint facial scar visible only when light catches it at a specific angle.
They have the walk of someone who has spent years on uneven terrain — a slight readiness in the knee and ankle.
Something in their stillness suggests they have sat through longer silences than this and will sit through longer ones again.
Their left eye is fractionally lower than their right — a subtle asymmetry that takes time to locate but explains the slight wrongness of their face.
A quality of taking up less space than their size would suggest — physically present but carefully contained.
They smell faintly of something medicinal — not unpleasant, just clinical.
A voice with an accent that places them somewhere specific, though they don't discuss where.
They measure things unconsciously — distances, quantities, time — and you catch them doing it.
A scar on the palm of their right hand, the kind that comes from catching a blade.
A habit of completing other people's sentences that is either warm or presumptuous depending on your tolerance.
A very short upper lip that gives their face a slightly unfinished quality.
A long upper lip that gives their face a somewhat patrician, serious quality at rest.
Eyes that reflect firelight unusually — a specific quality to it you couldn't quite explain.
The faint particular smell of a specific landscape — coastal air, pine forest, old stone — that clings to them.
A very defined philtrum — the ridge above the upper lip, prominent and deep.
A brow that furrows on one side more than the other, giving certain expressions an inadvertent quizzical quality.
One nostril slightly larger than the other — a feature that becomes apparent only after time spent with them.
A walking pace that adjusts precisely to whoever they're walking with, without appearing to do so.
Clothing worn in layers regardless of temperature — always at least three, never fewer.
A small deliberate scar on the back of their right hand — too clean to be accidental.
They introduce themselves with both a name and a trade or title, always, even when only one was asked for.
A quality of being difficult to photograph in memory — something about their features doesn't stick cleanly.
Eyebrows significantly lighter than their hair, giving their face an unintentionally surprised resting state.
They stand at a slight angle to whoever they're speaking to — never quite facing them head-on.
A voice that drops noticeably when they're being sincere and rises when they're performing.
A ring on their left hand worn on the wrong finger for its size — adjusted to fit, or deliberately placed.
Very even, slow speech — the same cadence whether they're describing the weather or delivering bad news.
A habit of finishing a drink before beginning any serious conversation, regardless of how full the cup is.
They go completely still when they're thinking hard — no fidgeting, no microexpressions, just a brief total absence.
